sections.paragraph: i.q. Arabic جَلسَ to sit, to sit down, to lie down. Son 4:1, “thy locks are as a flock of goats שֶׁגָּֽלְשׁוּ מֵהַר גִּלְעָד which lie down on mount Gilead.” Son 6:5. Prop. lie down, as if hanging from mount Gilead, from its side, see מִן No. 3 . Jerome 4:1, “quœ ascenderunt.” LXX. 6:4, Complut. ἀνέβησαν; compare جلس Conj. II. to go up.
